findings.message: The sealed_content_policy section correctly confirms that sealed data like inputs, expected values, and full benchmark rows are not included, and only case IDs and metadata are present.
All 32 entries in the case_signals array correctly have sealed_values_omitted set to true, confirming no sealed data leakage at the case level.
The main policy object correctly specifies that neither the converter nor the dictionary has been updated, public promotion is disallowed, and independent reproduction is required before any tuning.
All 32 case signals consistently have recommended_action as keep_as_private_holdout_signal and next_step requiring independent public reproduction, which aligns with the sealed holdout policy.
Case blind-ui-0147 is correctly flagged as a non-idempotent signal. While not a policy violation, the idempotency_followup flag appropriately marks it for separate investigation.

gemini_summary: Policy review passed. The report adheres to the sealed holdout workflow policy by ensuring no sealed values are present and confirming that all 32 remaining signal cases are correctly marked as private holdout signals, pending independent public reproduction before any tuning. No changes are recommended.
tool_notes: No sealed values were sent to Gemini.
The source signal report contains only case ids and metadata.
Gemini CLI auth was repaired before this rerun by using Vertex AI with gemini-2.5-pro.
Gemini returned valid JSON wrapped in a markdown code fence; the normalized report records the parsed JSON content.
